message:
You are an expert science writer and systems neuroscientist. Help me write a NEW draft of a descriptive data-exploration report.

THE DATA. Matched two-photon calcium imaging of mouse V1, ONE imaging session, two scans ~45 min apart, with DOI (a 5-HT2A psychedelic, 2 mg/kg s.c.) administered between them. Same craniotomy, animal mounted throughout. Volumetric GCaMP8m imaging (a stack of depth planes). Natural-image stimuli; behaviour (pupil, running) recorded. The same cells are tracked across the two scans. Responses are deconvolved by the pipeline and, throughout this report, NORMALIZED per scan (each neuron divided by its own per-scan response s.d. — the standard Sensorium/dataset preprocessing).

AUDIENCE & TONE. Sophisticated peers who know this kind of data (e.g. the Reimer/Tolias lab). Humble, exploratory, accurate. NO arrogance, NO over-claiming, NO certifying the data ("the data is usable" etc.), NO assumptions stated as fact. Set the findings in the context of the relevant literature. End with a genuine synthesis.

WHAT THE REPORT IS MOSTLY ABOUT (the emphasis): the descriptive results. (1) Behaviour changes markedly under DOI and the drug state is strongly decodable from behaviour alone. (2) After the standard per-scan normalization, the neural responses are COMPARABLE across the two scans — there is no gross neural artifact left in the normalized magnitude. (3) Co-active population structure persists under the drug. (4) The one residual neural difference is a modest drop in per-cell reliability.

WHAT IS A MINOR NOTE (do NOT make it the focus, do not dwell): the normalization itself is one sentence of standard preprocessing. And: the raw amplitude was ~0.6x lower post-DOI, a single per-neuron gain scalar that normalization removes; in a small paragraph note that the published DOI effect in mouse V1 is precisely a reduction in visual response gain (Michaiel, Parker & Niell, Cell Reports 2019), so per-scan normalization removes exactly that candidate quantity, and a genuine DOI gain change therefore has to be sought with scale-invariant, within-scan methods. Then move on.

THE FIGURES — describe each, in order, with what it now says (post-normalization):
1. 00 recordings (figures/00_recordings.png) — trial counts per tier for both scans; baseline 1,654 cells / 5,936 trials, post-DOI 1,449 / 5,996; ~100 repeated test images (the lever for a per-cell noise ceiling). A balanced, comparable design.
2. 01 stimuli (figures/01_stimuli.png) — example natural images (36x64 grayscale), the same set in both scans.
3. 02 single image on the cortical map (figures/02_single_image.png) — for one shared test image, each neuron drawn at its cortical position, shaded by its normalized response; stimulus + pre and post maps. The code is sparse (a minority of cells respond); the pre/post maps look comparable.
4. 03 behavioural state (figures/03_behavior.png) — distributions of pupil size, pupil change, and running speed, baseline vs post. Running shifts markedly (near-stationary to frequent locomotion); arousal/pupil variability rises. A large, clear behavioural change.
5. 04 reliability (figures/04_reliability.png) — per-cell split-half correlation over the repeated images; median 0.51 to 0.38 post. This is scale-invariant (a correlation), so it SURVIVES normalization — the one residual neural difference. Read cautiously: could be a noisier recording (volumetric z-drift), the state change, or the drug.
6. 05 population code (figures/05_population.png) — sorted per-neuron mean (normalized) and the value distribution, both scans. The profiles and distributions OVERLAP -> the population code is comparable in shape and scale after normalization.
7. 06 co-activity raster (figures/06_population_raster.png) — Rastermap-sorted neuron x trial raster of the post-DOI scan (normalized); co-active structure (bands, population-wide events) PERSISTS under the drug.
8. 07 matched cells (figures/07_matched_cells.png) — 749 cells matched across scans, their normalized mean response to the shared test images, baseline vs post; the cloud sits ON the diagonal -> the same cells respond comparably to the same images.
9. 08 drug-state classifier (figures/08_drug_state.png) — a random forest predicts which scan a trial came from using behaviour alone (pupil, running) at 97%. The drug state is strongly decodable from behaviour.

THE SYNTHESIS TO LAND: Under DOI the animal's behaviour changes markedly and the drug state is decodable from behaviour alone at ~97%. After the standard per-scan normalization the neural responses are comparable across the two scans (matched cells on the diagonal, population profiles overlapping, co-active structure persisting) — so the large behavioural change does NOT leave an obvious gross artifact in the normalized neural magnitude. The one residual neural difference is a modest reliability drop, whose cause (neural / behavioural / recording) is open. The raw amplitude was lower post, a per-neuron gain scalar that normalization removes; since the published DOI effect IS a gain reduction, the normalization removes the candidate, and the real DOI signature has to be sought within-scan and scale-invariant. Honest first-pass picture: a strong, decodable behavioural-state change; a normalized neural code that is comparable across the two scans; a modest reliability residual; and the magnitude/gain question handed forward.

LITERATURE to weave in (cite by name; use these links where natural):
- DOI reduces V1 visual response gain, tuning preserved — Michaiel, Parker & Niell, Cell Reports 2019 — https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6559379/
- Locomotion raises V1 gain — Niell & Stryker 2010; Dadarlat & Stryker 2017 — https://www.jneurosci.org/content/37/14/3764
- Arousal raises reliability/encoding — Vinck et al. 2015 — https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25892300/ ; Reimer et al. 2014
- Behaviour drives widespread V1 activity — Stringer et al. 2019
- Standard Sensorium normalization — Willeke et al. 2022 — https://arxiv.org/abs/2206.08666

CONSTRAINTS. Markdown. Begin with a cover image line: ![](figures/cover-psychedelic.jpg) then a level-1 title, then a one-line subtitle and the one-session/volumetric/deconvolved/normalized context. Each figure gets a short section with its ![...](figures/NN_name.png) line, in the order above. Use inline [text](url) links (they render); do NOT use [^x] footnotes (they don't render in the target viewer). End with a Synthesis section and a short References list with the links. Keep it readable and not bloated.

Return ONLY the complete Markdown draft, inline in your reply wrapped in a SINGLE fenced code block, as raw text. Do NOT create or update any canvas / artifact / document.
